commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2e5a31da8b21bbfe94c22193ce8d7ab36996ba4f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a state-repository-backed weighted provider lease that gates exact-review Codex invocation, reports capacity wait outcomes, and releases the lease before publishing review state.

Reproducibility: yes. at the source and simulation level: independent runs currently lack shared provider-capacity coordination, and the Git-backed tests reproduce competing acquisitions. The production provider overload and long-running expiration behavior have not yet been demonstrated in live Actions.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer should choose the lease-lifetime contract and review live Actions proof; missing real behavior proof also prevents an automated repair or merge path.

target.title: fix(review): gate exact reviews by provider lease
target.body:
## Why
ClawSweeper exact reviews can run from many GitHub workflow instances at once. Worker sizing only controls local/shard concurrency; it does not protect the shared Codex provider/API-key capacity across independent runs. The important behavior change in this PR is to move overload handling from "start Codex, hit provider capacity, then retry/fail" to "reserve a global provider slot before starting Codex."

That gives us a controlled queue/backpressure point inside ClawSweeper: while global provider capacity is full, an exact review waits for a lease and reports `Waiting for Codex capacity` instead of stampeding the provider and amplifying transient transport/capacity failures. If capacity remains full until the configured wait deadline, the workflow now writes a terminal `Capacity timeout` command status instead of leaving the command looking like it is still waiting. If the provider-lease state update itself fails, the run fails explicitly rather than pretending capacity is merely full.

## Summary
- add a state-repo-backed Codex provider lease tool with weighted capacity, TTL pruning, and acquire/release commands
- gate exact event reviews on a global provider lease before invoking Codex, then release before publishing state
- make current workflow capacity configuration authoritative over persisted lease state so emergency capacity changes take effect
- distinguish provider capacity waits, terminal capacity timeouts, and provider-lease state update failures in command status

## Testing
- `pnpm run build:repair`
- `pnpm run format:check`
- `node --test test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts`
- `node --test --test-name-pattern "event re-review status|provider capacity|provider lease" test/clawsweeper.test.ts`
- `autoreview --mode local` clean after fixes
- `pnpm run check` on Node v24.15.0

## Automated proof
The provider lease proof is intentionally CI-safe: it does not call Codex and does not touch production `openclaw/clawsweeper-state`. It uses a temporary local bare git repository as the shared state remote, then clones it into multiple independent checkouts to model multiple GitHub Actions workflow instances racing on the same `state` branch.

Code locations:
- shared-state proof test: [`test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L175-L295`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/2e5a31da8b21bbfe94c22193ce8d7ab36996ba4f/test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L175-L295)
- temp bare state repo setup: [`test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L310-L326`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/2e5a31da8b21bbfe94c22193ce8d7ab36996ba4f/test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L310-L326)
- independent state checkouts: [`test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L328-L332`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/2e5a31da8b21bbfe94c22193ce8d7ab36996ba4f/test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L328-L332)
- rejected push regression: [`test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L145-L173`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/2e5a31da8b21bbfe94c22193ce8d7ab36996ba4f/test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L145-L173)

What the shared-state proof executes:
- creates a temporary bare git origin and seeds its `state` branch
- clones that origin into two separate working copies to simulate independent workflow runners
- acquires `capacity=1` from the first checkout and verifies `acquired=true` with `activeWeight=1`
- attempts a second acquisition from another checkout and verifies `acquired=false`, `activeWeight=1`, and a `provider capacity full` reason
- releases the first lease and verifies a later acquisition can succeed
- creates an unreleased short-TTL lease, waits past TTL, then verifies the next acquisition succeeds and prunes the stale lease
- separately installs a rejecting `pre-receive` hook on the bare origin and verifies a failed shared-state push throws `shared state push failed` instead of returning a false successful acquisition

Observed results:
- `node --test test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts` passed locally: 7 tests, 7 pass, including the shared-state proof and rejected-push regression.
- `pnpm run check` passed locally on Node v24.15.0, including 453 unit tests, 502 repair tests, coverage checks, and format check.
- GitHub CI `pnpm check` passed on head `2e5a31da8b21bbfe94c22193ce8d7ab36996ba4f`: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/27398715298.
- GitHub CodeQL passed on the same head: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/27398715297.

What this proof does not claim:
- it is not real GitHub Actions concurrency against production `openclaw/clawsweeper-state`
- it does not prove cancellation recovery timing is operationally acceptable in production
- it does not prove the real Codex provider/API key will never throttle; it proves ClawSweeper's shared-state gate queues before invoking Codex when the configured lease capacity is full

## Live proof
- Pending: collect redacted live Actions evidence for concurrent acquisition, exhausted-capacity timeout, normal release, and cancellation/TTL recovery before final merge readiness.
